# Attribute namespace: `app`

<!-- DO NOT EDIT - AUTOGENERATED FILE. Regenerate with scripts/generate-docs.sh -->

| Attribute | Type | Stability | Description | Examples |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `app.action.end_type` | `enum` | development | Types of ending for an app action. |  |
| `app.environment` | `string` | development | The runtime environment in which the app is deployed. | `prod`, `dev`, `staging` |
| `app.nav.destination` | `string` | development | A human-readable string that uniquely identifies the destination of app navigation. | `Home Page`, `com.app.UserActivity`, `{userId} Profile` |


## `app.action.end_type` values

`app.action.end_type` has the following list of well-known values. If one of them applies, then the respective value MUST be used; otherwise, a custom value MAY be used.

| Value | Stability | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `abandon` | development | Action ended due to user abandonment |
| `error` | development | Action ended due to an application error |
| `success` | development | Action completed successfully |
| `unknown` | development | Action ended without confirmation of success, error, or abandonment |

## Notes

### `app.action.end_type`

Events that model app actions can specify this attribute to determine if it was successful or not. Any action event that omits this attribute is assumed to be successfully completed.

### `app.nav.destination`

This value SHOULD NOT contain high-cardinality dimensions like user ID. If such dimensions can be programmatically determined, they SHOULD be replaced with a generic token (e.g. `{userId} Profile` rather than the actual user ID).
